Operation 'getAvailablePaymentMethods' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is generic. Description lacks clarity on input requirements (basket value, merchant contract details), doesn't specify the types of payment methods returned, and doesn't explain the purpose for merchants. Enhanced version includes specific payment types from API context, clarifies inputs/outputs, and explains the checkout use case for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Returns the available payment methods for a specified basket value and merchant contract. Returns monthly installment amount, interest and fees.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve available BNPL payment methods for checkout
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves available BNPL payment methods (invoices, installments, direct debit) for a specified basket value and merchant contract. Requires basket amount and merchant contract details as input. Returns a list of available payment methods with associated costs including monthly installment amounts, interest rates, and applicable fees for each option. Helps merchants present payment options to customers during checkout.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description lacks clarity on input requirements (basket value, merchant contract details), doesn't specify the types of payment methods returned, and doesn't explain the purpose for merchants. Enhanced version includes specific payment types from API context, clarifies inputs/outputs, and explains the checkout use case for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'authorizePayment' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary lacks specificity about BNPL context. Description mentions payment types but omits critical details: what inputs are required (customer/order details), what is returned (authorization reference, order number), and the authorization lifecycle (hold without capture, requires subsequent capture). Enhanced version clarifies the authorization state, explains the two-step payment flow, and helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus capture.

data.current_description: Authorizes a payment transaction for BNPL payment methods, supporting multiple payment types including invoices, installments, and direct debit options.
data.description_suggestion: Authorizes a BNPL payment transaction for invoices, installments, or direct debit payment methods. Requires customer details, order information, and selected payment method as input. Returns authorization confirmation with order number and authorization reference. Creates a hold on the customer's payment method but does not capture funds. Authorization must be followed by capture operation to complete the transaction. Supports risk checking and validation of customer and order details.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks specificity about BNPL context. Description mentions payment types but omits critical details: what inputs are required (customer/order details), what is returned (authorization reference, order number), and the authorization lifecycle (hold without capture, requires subsequent capture). Enhanced version clarifies the authorization state, explains the two-step payment flow, and helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus capture.

message: Operation 'capturePayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specificity on inputs (order number, capture amount), outputs (transaction reference, status), and the partial capture workflow. Enhanced version clarifies the two-step authorization-capture flow, explains when to use partial captures, and helps AI agents understand the capture lifecycle and constraints.

data.current_description: Completes the payment that has been authorized. Typically done when the order is shipped. Can be a full or partial capture of the order amount.
data.description_suggestion: Completes a previously authorized BNPL payment by capturing funds. Requires the order number and capture amount as input. Returns capture confirmation with transaction reference and updated order status. Supports full or partial captures of the authorized amount. Typically performed when the order is shipped or fulfilled. Partial captures allow merchants to capture only the amount of items actually shipped, with remaining authorization available for additional captures or void.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specificity on inputs (order number, capture amount), outputs (transaction reference, status), and the partial capture workflow. Enhanced version clarifies the two-step authorization-capture flow, explains when to use partial captures, and helps AI agents understand the capture lifecycle and constraints.

message: Operation 'refundPayment' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate but description is too brief. Missing critical details: what inputs are required (order number, refund amount), what is returned (refund reference, status), refund constraints (only for captured payments), and the partial refund capability. Enhanced version clarifies the refund workflow, explains when refunds can be applied, and helps AI agents understand refund limitations and options.

data.current_description: Processes refunds for partially or fully captured payments.
data.description_suggestion: Processes refunds for captured BNPL payments. Requires the order number and refund amount as input. Returns refund confirmation with transaction reference and refund status. Supports full or partial refunds of captured amounts. Refunds can only be processed against previously captured payments. Partial refunds allow merchants to refund specific items or amounts while maintaining the remaining captured balance. Refunded amounts are returned to the customer's original payment method.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description is too brief. Missing critical details: what inputs are required (order number, refund amount), what is returned (refund reference, status), refund constraints (only for captured payments), and the partial refund capability. Enhanced version clarifies the refund workflow, explains when refunds can be applied, and helps AI agents understand refund limitations and options.

message: Operation 'voidAuthorization' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specificity on inputs (order number), outputs (void reference, status), and constraints (cannot void partially/fully captured payments). Enhanced version clarifies the void operation's effect on payment holds, explains when void can be used, and helps AI agents understand the authorization lifecycle and constraints.

data.current_description: Void (cancel) an authorization that has not been captured.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels a previously authorized BNPL payment that has not been captured. Requires the order number as input. Returns void confirmation with transaction reference and updated order status. Releases the hold on the customer's payment method. Can only be performed on authorized payments that have not been partially or fully captured. After void, the authorization cannot be captured and a new authorization must be created if payment is needed.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specificity on inputs (order number), outputs (void reference, status), and constraints (cannot void partially/fully captured payments). Enhanced version clarifies the void operation's effect on payment holds, explains when void can be used, and helps AI agents understand the authorization lifecycle and constraints.

message: Operation 'updateOrder' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specificity on inputs (order number, updated items), outputs (updated total, authorization adjustment), and important constraints/side effects (authorization adjustment for amount changes, cannot update fully captured orders). Enhanced version clarifies what can be modified, explains authorization implications, and helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation in the order lifecycle.

data.current_description: Modifies an existing authorized but not fully captured order. Allows merchants to add or remove items without generating a new order number.
data.description_suggestion: Modifies an existing authorized BNPL order by adding or removing items. Requires the order number and updated order items as input. Returns updated order confirmation with new order total and adjusted authorization amount if needed. Can only be performed on authorized orders that have not been fully captured. Allows merchants to adjust order contents without creating a new order number or requiring re-authorization. If order total increases, additional authorization may be required; if it decreases, the authorization is adjusted accordingly.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specificity on inputs (order number, updated items), outputs (updated total, authorization adjustment), and important constraints/side effects (authorization adjustment for amount changes, cannot update fully captured orders). Enhanced version clarifies what can be modified, explains authorization implications, and helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation in the order lifecycle.
